11 years ago was close to 230 pounds. Today I range about 145. I've not worked out nor went to the gym. I changed my diet and became more conscious of what and how much I eat. And yet I still feel unaccomplished. Many years of bullying has left my mind still thinking I'm unattractive

When I was first starting my fitness journey a few years back, I told my friends that I couldn't wait for the day where I looked the same standing up as I do laying down (when I laid down, I'd look more muscular). It's crazy but I think I'm there now - in fact, I look even more toned standing up

Friday morning, early wake up, down to the basement gym for a full body lift, keep that momentum going into the weekend and finish the week strong. Mara hung out with me this morning. Seeing improvements in multiple areas, finally breaking through the plateau, just had to keep working for it.

I have finally achieved my desired waist to height ratio (even during PMS) and I’m so pleased. Weight gets meaningless when you work with heavy objects in your routines, but that ratio fills me with joy 🥰

Yesterday at work one of my coworkers told me that's it's astonishing how much weight I've lost and that it's really noticable. I don't see it myself sometimes but I suppose other people can really tell. I guess losing 100 lbs will do that. 😅

I did one of those keto “cleanses” a year ago, and the only habit I’ve kept from it is not putting sugar in my tea.

It sucked. What do you mean, I can’t eat a APPLE? Who the hell do you think you are? Yahweh?

I lost like eight pounds in six weeks, and it sucked.

I jumped through hoops, I cried to my psych and my therapist, I got lucky and Dark and I got insurance thru the state which covered it

I lost 100 lbs!!!!! From 340 to 240!!! I changed my diet, I exercised a lot, I have been working thru my ptsd and my coping mechanisms
It was hard
#SunnyGRSJournal

My work-from-home setup has a motorized sitting/standing desk and my "chair" is a yoga ball which has been amazing for my lower back and fidgeting tendencies.

But it's also the prop I recorded for Baymax's body squeaks in Big Hero 6! So basically legacy sounds every time I move heh

Going to the gym on a Friday night is bad enough without having to wait for all the dudes trying to get a pump going before the club to clear out of the place. Mind you, I recognize this may be a problem unique to West Hollywood

heres what sucks: trying to feel good as a secondary effect of exercise
heres whats cool: getting exercise as a secondary effect of feeling good

I MADE IT TO THE GYM! 

Quite the adventure on crutches & in a moon boot, I have to say. 🤣 

Didn't do a lot, but better than nothing. 

Chest press
Bicep curls
Shoulder press
Back extensions
Pecs

I skipped the rear delts. 

The lat pulldown & seated row I thought a bit dicey yet, given the foot.

In my yoga practice, I'm familiar with instructors talking about the balance between effort and ease, and (maybe not surprisingly) I'm much more of an effort person. But today at church, I felt the ease of this vocation in a way I don't usually, which was a good feeling.

Ten PRs and 32,000 kg moved. I am a happy bear today.

When I started the gym two years ago I had no idea I would enjoy it as much as I do.

Those that know me from the other place may recall that 3 months ago I started Slimming World. 
I’ve since lost 20kg and my chest measurement has come down 28cm, and my waist 8cm. 
My physical and mental health has improved mightily. If you can find the motivation, do it! 
You won’t be sorry.

Attended my first ever yoga class today. I felt affirmed, centered, grateful, and even powerful at some moments. It was so good. Afterward, I was in such a great mood. It’s rare for me to get that “endorphin rush” after exercise but I definitely felt it. Gonna be sore tomorrow but I’ll be back!

I think I've posted this before, but apparently I'm going through old ultimate frisbee photos. It turns out playing or practicing sports almost every day of the week because that's the only time you're comfortable in your body will make you kinda swole

We played as mimes, silently
I’m down more than 30 pounds now: no sugar, no red meat, no processed food, minimal dairy. I feel better than I have in years. I’d like to lose 10 more.

I weight lift because it's fun, great for women's health and helps tremendously with my C-PTSD. 

Last night I was able to add another reason to my list, as I found two passed out/puking women on the sidewalk and physically carried them up the stairs to their apartment.
I have recently gotten back to a regular yoga practice after some time away, and it is so nice to be doing an activity where I am neither the best nor the worst person in the room
Body composition aside, my vo2 and recovery rate are increasing. That’s more important, and I’m glad for it. Keep on running Dax.

Holy shit. I actually fucking did it! Double digits, 30.15kg (66.5lb) lost, goal reached.

I haven't weighed this little since I was in high school. Early high school. 😭

Today, I leg pressed 80kg. That's like a person! (Don't @ me gym rats, for me that's a lot). 
I also had apple crumble for lunch because it's all about balance.🥧

is there some level of exercise intensity that improves joint issues if exertion makes ur joints hate u? i've been extremely low exertion since i got covid and sprained my elbow, and want to ramp back up
After a full week of counting calories, I can safely say I’m shocked at both how easy it is to stay under your calorie limit and how easy it is to drastically exceed your calorie limit.
The elevator in my office building says the weight limit is 3500 lbs OR 21 persons and I’m just wondering how many times 21 adult Americans would be at or below 3500 pounds.

I’d say it was a successful first in my weight loss journey. I was fasting for most of the day. I did a solid hour on he stairmaster and dinner was a fairly small, but high protein salad. It’s a bit daunting, but this isn’t the first time I’ve had to drop a lot of weight.
turns out if you start exercising regularly and then skip a day it can crater your mood!!! the archvillain known as The Human Body always gotta have you addicted to SOMETHING

Got VERY close to beating my all time best deadlift today (it's tougher this time around because now it's 50 pounds over my bodyweight), but didn't get all the way.

SOON.

Always impressed how a workout with lighter weights plus good control and higher rep ranges can be so effective. 

Did a leg workout with 1/5th the weight I usually use and I'm really feeling it!

After a month and a half in the warehouse I've definitely noticed that my body is beginning to lose weight and tone up. I almost kind of want to join the YMCA and keep lifting weights. Access to the locker room is pure bonus lol.
If you have some dumbbells lying around or can safely improvise the weight then this workout will make you strong in your body and your brain.

the best part about working out is that when you do some task which requires using your muscles, like sanding the grinding grit off a piece of sheet metal, you find out that real world tasks use some other special set of muscles exercising can’t reach

exercise is great tho even though it's not great for weight loss - maybe because it is? like exercise is an incredible mental boost and will make you feel better in all facets of life, even if it's tough to get into a routine

Just got on the scale for the first time since December. Figured I’d see myself trimming down after a month of no booze, no dairy, no processed carbs, and no sugar. A month of solid sleep, steady workouts, and general good habits.

Number came in heavy as fuck. No idea how.

With the fox starting to feel better andy tattoo healing, headed back to the gym next week. 

Hella nervous because I haven't been back since I had my stroke. Hopefully a year break was good for healing.

Depending on that, I might be looking at getting a powerlifting gym membership early next year.
It's funny how a year can make a big difference in mental health and how you feel about yourself. Also being 60+ lbs heavy definitely helps.

When you drag your teenager to the gym at 6am with you and she's not happy you woke her up early.

But after the workout she tells you "you are not the same mama she use to know......you are amazing. And thank you for everything you do. 

No better feeling.

You wanna know something cool they don't tell you about working out?
You can do the stuff you want to and you don't really have to do the stuff you hate.
If no one has told you yet and the idea appeals to you- you are allowed to lift things and get strong and muscular.
Women too. Especially women.

So far, the main results I’ve noticed from working out is that my back is unexpectedly toned? And just watching how insufferable that’s made me is all but guaranteeing I’m two months away from becoming the worst person you know.

Finally back under 200lbs for the first time in prolly about 2 years.
Been feeling a lot better. Hopefully I can keep the momentum up.

Throwback, spring 2013. 2013 was the year I did a lot of working out but no particular focus on eating habits. In effect, my last year (so far) of being a big boy and my first of being a fitness nut.

It was a pretty darn good year.

Now, I’m not saying you HAVE to work out on Xmas. But if you do, you get a solidarity fisting… err, fist bump! I brought my push-up board and resistance bands, so I get to work out on the go. Calisthenics travels well.

Down another pound this morning to 154. I started this year at 163, to give a sense of how much effort has gone into reaching just this small point. 

It's come down to exercising every day, working more on my diet, and just taking it all a step at a time

Celebrating being down to the 230s (almost 120 pounds lighter than my heaviest point ten years ago) for the first time in two decades by wearing my new shirt. I've wanted one of these my whole life. IYKYK

Worked out for the first time in about five weeks since getting WhateverTheFuckVirus slayed me, and it felt so good. A pleasantly painful, maybe gonna throw up, shakey-all-over workout. 

I’m back!! 🥰

I found my old swim trunks today and threw them on for the heck of it. May 2014 vs today. And I'm actually up about 20 lbs from my way-too-low weight last summer.

That...kinda helped!

Decided I need to do more rowing and deadlifts. D’you know, I haven’t done a single deadlift at this gym and we’ve been members nearly two years!Used to do them loads as well.
No wonder I just don’t feel thicc anymore! 

That’s about as new-year resolutiony as I get!
My first time doing arm day at the gym since Kid started Christmas break and I guess my 100 push-ups every other day thing is paying off because I actually ended up going up on the free weights.

today has been a really good day. I have been able to achieve the things I wanted including doing a decent exercise session. I need exercises and stretches to keep my knees going (osteo athritis) and I have been in a fug with it. Feeling like I am back on track with it this week. yay me

Been really feeling myself since changing my diet. I've lost 45 lbs since my peak weight in early 2022 and I'm at a comfortable 180 lbs now ^^

Dick looks bigger, belly getting flatter, ass still fat af~ ✨️
Every time I decide to have a daily yoga practice I immediately feel better about all things and tbh that’s kind of rude
Losing weight through fitness vs my previous relationship to it, which was disordered eating and man it's like night and day I feel great after riding my fucked up bike
hate how they were right and if you can get into the rhythm of exercise for long enough it starts to feel amazing and you miss it when you're not doing it
Non-gaming post for a change, I've managed to drop about 20lbs in a couple of months. It's been very boring, but you can't argue with the results.

Today’s shoulder pump was JUUUUUIIIIICCCCCCY!!!!! Was great to be back at it after a few days off. It’s okay to take a few days off from the gym btw. It won’t immediately lose your gainz. Sometimes ya just need a little break if ya been going at it super hard :)

Good morning. 🏋🏽‍♀️☕️

Chasing some more leg health today - also crossing my fingers that I can get one of those mechanical monitor/desk things at work that let me stand up and sit down whenever I need to. Using the half-yoga ball chair has definitely helped, too.

the best caramelized onions you will ever make:

4-5 onions of various kinds, sliced 

5 tbsp salted butter

In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions and mix. Turn heat to low. Cook for 4-5 hours, till onions are browned and sweet, with an almost jam-like texture.

Pasta alla gricia is an amazing old school dish in the "dry" class (ie dressed, not sauced). Very simple, it includes only the pasta, guanciale, pepper, water, and Pecorino Romano. It is rich, indulgent, and delicious.

Mom made… well, I’m loath to call it paella as its not got Chicken/Chorizo, but it has paella rice, prawns, hake, wild salmon, garlic, peppers etc & a 1/4 bottle of white wine, stock, last night’s lamb fat. We soaked the saffron.  Calling it a seafood risotto wouldn’t irk the purists. #Homecooking

Duck fat fries with garlic dip. (Dip’s a little fresh garlic, chipotle sauce, and 50/50 mayo/Greek yogurt.) 🍜🍽️

Made in the air fryer. Secret is: starchy potato cut thinner, & soaked in cold water, with a rinse. Dry them well before fat/cooking, & brush w more fat 3/4 through cooking them.

Pappardelle coccolare, or Coddle pasta in Hiberno English. You need a good few fatty rashers and sausages here. Make a normal carbonara sauce w two extra egg yolks for a thicker sauce and some sharp white Dubliner cheese grated in. give it a good mix. Parsley to garnish and white pepper to serve.

Schweinshaxe (ham hocks), served with roasted vegetables, potato gratin rosettes, and a beer gravy.

The rub on the ham hocks is salt, pepper, juniper berries, carraway and fennel seeds.

It's even better than it looks. 🍜🍽️
I don't know who needs to hear this but two parts tahini to one part pickle juice (by weight), whisked together with a little water, makes a shockingly good little tahini sauce

Afgan mantu (pronounced mantwo) are usually made with lamb, but pumpkin is a main ingredient in Afghan cuisine and I wanted mine with a version of borani kaddo: heavily spiced chunks of roasted pumpin and onion. The dumplings and sauces (yogurt, turmeric tomato, herby green chile) are plant-based.

This is my new favourite way to make potatoes - steam until soft, then partially crush in a baking tray and drizzle with olive oil and a pinch of salt, then roast for about 90 min. You can also pour over some vinegar before roasting for salt and vinegar potatoes which goes really well with fish 😋

Made the Short Rib Onion Soup from Smitten Kitchen yesterday. You spend 2.5 hours braising bone-in short ribs and—in the process—make the world’s best double beef stock, and while that’s happening you caramelize a shitton of onions and season them with sherry. Last: mix and top with Gruyère toasts!

lao ganma (“old godmother”) is a chinese brand of fried chili crisp condiments (deep fried chili flakes in oil). it is a potent flavor enhancer

white rice + spoonful of lao ganma + fried egg on top is a class chinese college dorm room meal

Beef on Weck. Roast beef on a kummelweck roll, a roll that is topped with kosher salt and caraway seeds. The meat on the sandwich is traditionally served rare, thin cut, with the top bun getting a dip in jus and spread with horseradish.

I've also made it with venison backstrap - yum!
Sri Lankan cabbage and carrot curry. Cooked with cashews, lots of spices, and coconut milk. Served with a tangy lime and coconut sambal.

PSA: a tagine does brilliant roasts, especially beef. Doesn't broil them like a slow cooker or dry them out like a roasting  dish. Tonight's bolar roast was melt in the mouth tender. Coated in pomegranate molasses, salted lime and fermented garlic.

I take some pride in my home-made nacho game. 

I make queso with a roux, Monterey Jack, Velveeta, and chipotles in adobo, then layer it with meat, (leftover steak or chorizo is best), fresh and pickled jalapeños and a combo of salsa verde and nacho Doritos (or whatever)

Broil then add toppings.

A salmon cake made with saltines is a beautiful thing (like a crab cake but with salmon!), why on EARTH would you ketchup it when there’s an entire universe of more interesting and helpful condiments available to you

I spent a lot of my life thinking I hated soup - seemed like it was just a bland, watery grave for overcooked vegetables and pasta. But making soup from scratch at home completely changed my opinion! This intensely flavorful pasta e fagioli with fresh crusty rolls is one of my favorite meals. ❤️

What a difference marinating and stewing down makes.

Filipino Adobo is one of my favorite dishes, if I've got the spoons to make it.

For the past five years, it's been what I make on Christmas, because family traditions have to start somewhere.

Orange, green olive, red onion, a sardine, and some chili flakes. Less than €10 at a sit-down restaurant, and one of the best things I ate in Sicily. An object lesson in humbling all preconceptions before the majesty of fresh simple local ingredients.

Two things people do wrong with French toast:

One, use thin bread. Yuck. Should be 3/4” thick, or 1”. 

Two, don’t soak it long enough. I’ll be giving my bread an hour to soak up all the egg. If you soak too short, the inside of the bread gets no egg or flavour. 🍜🍽️

This dish isn’t terribly photogenic, which is a shame because it’s seriously delicious so I hope you’ll give it a chance. It’s a Greek dish called gigantes plaki. Dried lima beans are baked in a garlicky tomato sauce until they are velvety soft and divinely flavorful.

Yesterday’s beans and tortillas got turned into crispy tostadas and topped with scrambled eggs with green onions and the salsa verde from making my carnitas. 

I just rub corn tortillas with ghee and start crisping them in a pan then finish them in a toaster oven before topping ‘em. 🍜
Peruvian tiradito made with yellowtail our friend fished. I spent a ton of time carefully arranging the fish and avocado slices, but you can't tell with all of the sauce poured on 😮‍💨. 🍜

Back on my savoury porridge bullshit and can confirm that making the porridge with miso soup paste and stirring through some peanut rayu is probably the best idea I'll have in 2024. This one is topped with kimchi fried tofu and soft boiled egg and is the lunch of champions.

Déjeuner au Pavillon des Boulevards à Bordeaux, une étoile au Michelin, loin du tape-à-l'œil de la place de la Comédie où l'animateur de télé et parfois cuisinier français fait face à son homologue anglais.
On attaque le menu du samedi avec un pied de cochon frit, purée et pickles de céleri. 1/n
Iron cheffing the fridge for lunch. Toasted baguette with thin sliced NY cheddar, prosciutto, goat cheese, slices of ripe peaches.  Eaten with a bowl of salmorejo. PoliSkyCooks

I come before you again with more adulations for the veggie burger at Ted’s Montana Grill. 
It’s vegan, though I am not, and the flavor combinations in this thing are incredible.
Herbs, spices, and a soft texture that is moist, but not greasy. 
It doesn’t taste like a BURGER.
It just tastes GOOD.
Lemon fettuccine with a lemon, butter, garlic, white wine sauce. Lots of Parmigiano-Reggiano on top. Not exactly pretty but absolutely delicious.

Re: bland foods Here in Madagascar we have a trademark dish that’s just a few herbs boiled in a lot of water, but we harbor no illusions as to whether it’s tasty: it’s named “romatsatso” (“blandbroth”).

🇲🇬

I've been making versions of my mom's tortellini pasta salad since I left home, always with bottled creamy cesar even though I'm grossed out by bottled salad dressing. Today I realized I could put oil, vinegar, and a little Dijon on there instead and lmao its so good

A salad hot (correct) take: 

If you are making a salad with kale, cranberries, goat cheese, and candied walnuts, do not under any circumstances also make the dressing sweet. Goat cheese is not enough savory/tangy on its own to deal with all that. This salad is like eating the saddest candy bar :(
Seared Duck breast over roasted root vegetable and wheat berries. Orange balsamic gastrique with caramelized onions, dried figs and cherries

Had some pals over yesterday for EDH Boxing League brunch and made stuffed French toast filled with talegio cheese, prosciutto, and a fig and mustard spread. Also made home fried potatoes w/onions and garlic and Luger’s bacon. Served here with Calabrian chile maple syrup.

I wrote about this fancy sandwich. It's a crispy hash brown patty, custardy square of egg, melty gouda, bacon, pepper jelly, and pickled onion aioli sandwiched between one of my home-baked soft potato buns. Go read about it here: boundedbybuns.com/go-go-breakf...

So I've been experimenting cooking with powdered vinegar, and tonight I added it and kosher salt to potatoes roasting in pork fat,

And I expected something like 'salt and vinegar chips,'

But instead what I got was 'fucking irresistible perfectly balanced crispy potatoes.'

Two of my favourite Bangladeshi dishes which evoke memories of childhood - “aloo satni”, potatoes mashed with raw diced onion, fried chillies and garlic, mustard oil, fresh coriander and “aloo bhazi”, finely chipped potatoes stir fried with onion, garlic, chilli, fresh coriander

Omelette stuffed w squash blossoms, tomatillos, shiitakes, cheese spread (grated cheddar mixed w herbed feta). Seasoned w rosemary, paprika, black truffle salt. The sliced avocado has scorpion salt (yes, real scorpions) #FoodIsGood

Polished off the salad and the salmon tataki has arrived: as described, cooked on the outside and rare inside, sliced into chunks, coated with sesame seeds, served on a bed of chopped iceberg lettuce, cherry tomato halves, sliced mushrooms (shitake I think), seared pineapple chunks, & teriyaki sauce

My new favorite way to make potatoes. Get the small variety.

Boil them for 15 minutes (start preheating yoir air fryer after 10).

Drain them and let the water steam off, then toss them in some butter or oil and salt.

Air fry them on 400 for 10 minutes. Toss them halfway through.

Serve and enjoy.

I’m making duck fat fries. I brined handcut Russett potatoes in boiling water with pickling vinegar & sea salt, rinsed & dried them. 

They should get crispier but also fluffier & have a bit of zing n salt to them. I shall report. 🍽️🍜

The well-seasoned steak shall now saute in duck fat too. 🦆

Cut up your jack-o-lantern, throw your spice rack (and a little brown sugar) at it, roast it, and then put a nice hanger steak on top. Simple, satisfying and yet at the same time a provocative mashup of various old and new worlds. Also just a nice break from potatoes. Eat them pumpkins!
Mushroom Ragout served over Polenta. Two pounds of  various mushrooms cooked down with herbs, garlic, wine, and brandy. A nice 2019 Lirac from southern Rhone region. #Mmmm 🍜🍽️

This hotel has three types of tacos:

- fried shrimp, with a mild and tasty avocado salsa

- carne asada, with a delicious and spicy tomato and jalapeno salsa

-carnitas, with a scotch bonnet-based salsa that’s like licking a volcano

Burger King Steakhouse XT (2010-2012): A seasoned, "extra thick" beef patty, with cheese, lettuce and tomato, on a cornmeal dusted bun. Available in "Smoky Cheddar" (cheddar cheese, bacon, and bbq sauce) and "A.1" (American cheese, mayo, A1 steak sauce & crispy onions)

"Can you make me something Szechuan-ish for lunch so i feel more alive again?" 

Charred napa cabbage, fried egg, bacon. Sauce based on crispy chili, szechuan peppercorn, sesame oil. 🍽️🍜

Just saw a sausage gravy recipe that called for pouring off the sausage grease and then making the roux with butter. You do you but I’m just adding flour to that grease because it’s already there, hot, and full of sausage flavor. 🍜🍽️

Flatbread for dinner! A no-knead dough piled with a mighty heap of halved cherry tomatoes, Kalamata olives, quartered artichoke hearts, sliced mushrooms, diced garlic, sliced red bell pepper, finely diced broccoli, and sliced fresh basil. Side of spicy marinara for dipping.
Cheeseburger salad. Romaine, tomato, pickles, ground beef with Montreal steak spice, marble cheddar, croutons, and Big Mac special sauce dupe thinned with pickle juice as dressing.
Lamb breast with sauce tranche of cilantro, rice wine vinegar, red pepper flake, and pomegranate molasses atop orange-scented rice with cumin-roasted carrots.

dude this was fucking great. absolutely flawless execution of the fundamentals of macaroni and cheese. the breadcrumbs not as a topping but as a thickener after combining the milk and cheese totally transform it. the top would have come out crispier too but i had to turn off the oven early
A reprise of last night’s dinner: white truffle cheddar and my homemade orange-rhubarb marmalade, toasted on an everything bagel, dusted with cayenne, and sprinkled with fresh rosemary.
Braised long bean noodles with doubanjiang and lamb Merguez sausage instead of the usual pork strips/bacon ROCKS. Spices in the sausage play really surprisingly well with the bean paste!
Local baguette (Tall Grass), local hot mustard (Elman’s), Brunswick sardines in spring water, local pickled red onions (St-Léon Gardens). Delicious! 🍜 🍽️

Picked up a shoulder of lamb from Otway Prime and cooked it. It’s the simplest possible thing — an onion, eight garlic cloves, white wine, stock and a tiny splash of pasatta — probably less than a tablespoon. That’s it. Really not a lot of liquid. Five hours at about 140. So good!!

Go-to quick and easy comfort food.
Spanish Granja beans (butterbeans). Onions. Spicy chorizo. Canned tomatoes. Oregano.
You don't need a recipe. Just chuck it all in and let simmer. Fry the chorizo a little and soften the onions in that first.
A bowl of cheer-you-up.
You're welcome.

once again, i would like to promote the homemade Taco Bell potatoes: create a 50/50 flour and corn starch dredge, season with all your favorite southwest dried spices and salt to taste, toss diced potatoes to coat, toss in cooking oil, convection roast until crispy. no par-boiling needed.

A pretty wholesome stir fry of tofu, baby corn, carrot, onion & courgette on jasmine rice 🌱 🍽️ 🍜

(seasoned with black bean, garlic, ginger and chili; topped with coriander, spring onions & sesame seeds 🌶️ 🧄 🫚)

Fried egg sandwich, egg was fried in steak fond and seasoned with white pepper and mexican oregano, on toasted sourdough with a sauce made from 1tsp mayonnaise, 1tsp crema, glug of olive oil with powdered garlic and italian seasoning. Blowin’ my tits off ova here

Friday Kitchen Fuckery-

Homemade crispy Parmesan polenta cakes, over a roasted garlic and cherry tomato sauce, sautéed broccoli rabe with lemon, garlic, and red pepper flakes, and shrimp with dill walnut pesto.

Bolognese update: the first picture is how it looks after letting it simmer for several minutes. This is good: we want the fat to separate. It gets stirred back in before we serve it, but this means it is working. Second pic is how it looks after a stir. We’re close. Just one hour.

successful replication of my favorite breakfast dad used to make. feta and izmir tulumu (a salty, dense, tangy sheep's milk cheese from my hometown) mixed with an egg, parsley, pepper, spread over thick slices of white bread broiled in the toaster oven. tastes exactly like my childhood 🥰
You want quality leftover action? How about thinly sliced prime rib, cheese, and stuffing served on lightly toasted marbled rye with mayo and Coleman’s mustard?

Marinated some real cheap steaks in pureed guajillo roasted peppers/ adobo chiles with some lemon juice, salt pepper, soy sauce, cumin

braised in oven, this time we seared in pan afterwards to have with corn torillas and quick pickle onions.. . . . .. . . .

ok I also made a little batch of bbq sauce

as far as Bang For Buck in the kitchen, homemade BBQ sauce is right there with fruit jam as the simplest thing to make that's 1000x better than anything you can buy in a store, at any price

I made salted salmon onigiri and pickled watermelon rind! Perfect for a hot day like this: the salmon took 6 minutes in the toaster oven, the rice cooker generates almost no heat, and the watermelon isn’t cooked at all.

Pickled red onions. Huzzah. It’s a riff on what Bobby Flay always makes — except I’m cheap & I use white vinegar instead of red wine vinegar with grenadine. 

Flay never adds anything past RWV/grenadine but I added a bay leaf, pinch salt/MSG, & several pink peppercorns. Keeps for a few months.🍜🍽️

Malagasy vocabulary:

Laoka: a side dish that’s served with rice.

A “normal” Malagasy meal is supposed to be rice with laoka. Many Malagasy people are willing to accept a meal with very little laoka if necessary, but a meal with not enough rice is simply Not a True Meal.

🇲🇬
Breakfast - pulled pork rendang eggs Benedict. Hollandaise on poached eggs with slow cooked pulled pork rendang, citrus mustard salad with strawberry slices on a bed of coconut rice

Any time I find beef tongue on special I will always buy it. It's my second favourite part of the head, cheeks as number one.
Parboiled with citrus fruits, garlic, onions, celery, fresh herbs, salt and pepper.
Then sliced thin, dry rubbed, and seared. 🍜

My Sunday Supper was lemon garlic pork loin filet over Tuscan white beans with tomatoes and greens served with a flatbread on the side. I used a little bit of smoked pimenton and balsamic in the beans and finished with a tiny bit of parmesan on top. Very good, and I feel it was pretty healthy too.
Here we have a katsu curry burger! Crispy fried chicken seasoned with Japanese togarashi spice. Thick spicy Japanese curry sauce. Shredded cabbage, pickle and tomato on a wholewheat bun.

learned on accident that too many scobies in a kombucha brew makes it tastes vinegary. learned subsequently i can marinate and dehydrate the old scobies into jerky. what a forgiving homecraft. here’s them marinating

Not sure what to call this. Lazy porridge recipe!

- 125g salted butter
- 250g oat groats
- 1L water
- 0.75L milk
- 1 tsp salt

1. Fry the oats in butter, salt
2. Pour in water, cook in rice cooker on "brown rice" setting
3. Pour in milk, leave on "keep warm" setting
4. Eat when chewy & delicious (~6hrs)

Hot sauce taxonomy:

Yellow: Caribbean; mustard, vinegar, pepper

Chili-Garlic: Sriracha defines the type, but is its most mid member

Cholula-Tapatio: Classic hot sauce, more pepper than vinegar, often with some garlic

Tabasco et al: mostly vinegar

Beliezian: Like Cholula/Tapatio, but with carrot

Kinshi tamago, also known as thinly shredded egg omelette, is a delightful Japanese culinary art. It involves cooking a thin omelette, then slicing it into delicate, narrow strips. These silky strands add a beautiful touch to various dishes and an interesting textural contrast.

How to make Greek style roasted lemon chicken with potatoes!

How to make a good red sauce:

3. Cook your tomato paste!!!
4. Lots of fresh garlic
5. Season throughout cooking not just at the end
6. Use a wooden spoon

Next up: the Texas yellowstar, native to Texas; Oklahoma; Arkansas; through to Coahuila. This pretty annual herb is commonly found in prairie-like habitats across north-central and southern east Texas, specifically along the Edwards Plateau. It does best in well-draining sand and limestone. 🌿⭐️

Singleaf piñon, Pinus monophylla, is the only single-needle pine - all others have 2-5 needles in bundles. It has large seeds, piñones, that are delicious. Piñones are important to Native Americans throughout the  range of the tree. This is an old tree on the side of a canyon near Petrified Forest.

In 1960, David Latimer planted a garden inside of a bottle and sealed it shut. He opened the bottle and watered the plant in 1972 and sealed it for good. It has been a self sustaining ecosystem for 60 years.

The Texas mountain laurels are officially in full bloom! Not a true mountain laurel, it’s a small shrub-like evergreen in the pea family endemic to central and west Texas through northern Mexico. Their blooming season (March through April) fills the air with a STRONG smell resembling grape soda! 🌿

Today's Gardening Advice: Need a flowering tree for a small space? This is Cercis canadensis 'Covey', also sold as 'Lavender Twist.' Flowers form all up and down the stems and the weeping form reaches about 10'. Prefers full sun to partial shade.  They don't tend to do well with dry soil/hot sun. 🌱

My primary reason for being on Madeira was hoping to encounter one of the rarest native European orchids, Madeiran endemic Goodyera macrophylla. Only a few dozen plants are known to flower annually, and in dangerously inaccessible places. Seeing this plant was a moment of sheer euphoria.

Potatoes were cultivated by indigenous people across the Americas, but likely 1st domesticated in the Andes region of Peru 7-10,000 years ago from wild species Solanum brevicaule.

Cultivated potatoes are all tetraploid, while wild potatoes can be diploid, tetraploid, or hexaploid.

it’s the first wildflower of spring, although it doesn’t look like it! 🌿

Symplocarpus foetidus | skunk cabbage is a wetland plant that can generate their own heat and are the first eastern plant to bloom each year. The stinky little dome flowers are pollinated by flies.

the blooms of Cistus ladanifer look as though they're made of crumpled silk; they last only one day, but are produced in long succession. I would still grow it if it didn't bloom at all simply for the pervasive, sweet amber fragrance of the foliage.

The first California native wildflower to bloom from seed in my newly-created meadow section in my yard, Nemophila menziesii or Baby Blue Eyes. It’s a part of the Japanese-inspired native garden I’ve been working on for the last year after removing the lawn. 🌱

The precious Paperwhite Narcissus grows well in Florida, as long as you give it a cooler location to live in. Being one of the few varieties that doesn’t need chill to flower, they will bloom in clusters of showy, fragrant petals. Bulbs can be planted in either soil or a rock bed.

Recovering from learning:
1) Pineapples grow from shrubs (not trees)
2) The plant is a perennial and can produce multiple fruits, each taking about a year to grow.
3) Wild pineapples are pollinated mostly by hummingbirds.
4) Of course, there's messed up colonial history of pineapple monoculture.

This snazzy little flower is a rain lily, Zephyranthes atamasca is native to a chunk of the Southeast but threatened in some of its range. I planted them lining the walkway in the back. They look like a clump of chives most of the year, but after a good rain, they flower (hence the name.)

my greatest plant discovery to date, a county record for Iris verna | dwarf iris 🌿

a truly tiny plant with full iris flowers that loves sandy places. I found this, the bigger of two populations in Wise County, VA, in an old forest fire site that brimmed with botanical wonders

Question for the gardening folk: last year, my cosmos grew tall before they flowered—the ones on the sunnier patch got as tall as me and flowered pretty late into fall. This year, they have been staying much smaller, but blooming earlier. What might be the reason for this difference? 🌱

The creek disappears into a dark tree-cave at the back of my property. Last year while clearing nearby brush I went in, shocked to find pieces of hydrangeas clinging to the sandy, pebbled banks. Their stems/roots were mostly petrified driftwood. I relocated 5. 4 have recovered. 2 have bloomed! 🌱 🌿

Today's Gardening Advice: If you're looking for a deep red daylily, Buckyballs is an excellent choice.  The flowers are extremely dark and the foliage tends to be evergreen in warmer climates. It's not especially picky about soil (like most daylilies) but does prefer full sun.  🌱

We had a lemon tree on the balcony that outgrew its pot. We gave it to the caretaker, who planted it in the garden of our block of flats. This year it’s groaning under the weight of dozens of lemons so I’ve just been down to relieve it of part of its burden. The smell in our kitchen is unbelievable.

Growing dahlias from seed is a crap shoot, at best, but I adore the uncertainty of it. It makes every seedling a revelation.

This one came from a Yellow Cherish seed 🤷‍♀️.

You don't necessarily propogate your seed plants, but I really like this one and I'll probably save her tubers if they come.🌱

I know it’s hella naive to say this, but at this point, I feel like I’ve grown every tomato 🍅 San Marzano, the Cherokees, the Rainbows…

And yet, nothing comes close to these two 🏆 B.K. is a vigorous shade/cold-tolerant heirloom with notes of thyme and oregano 🌱 and Sungold needs zero introduction.
🌱 Second year growing these dwarf “cosmic orange” sulphur cosmos and they are as fantastic as ever. Incredibly bright orange, floriferous, easy care—can’t ask for a better late-summer annual.

I have been cataloguing my thistles today. This is Carduus nutans, Musk Thistle or Nodding Thistle, a plant of pasture, roadside verges, & other grassy habitats with us in North Yorkshire. Has a fragrance of musk, or almonds. Blooming in August.

Cosmos is the flower for the birth month of October and symbolizes balance and harmony.It attracts butterflies and is easily used as an attractive border or backdrop plant.Cosmos is simple and easy to grow from seed sown in the spring, readily reseeds itself,and tolerates a wide range of soil types.

Cardinal Basil! A highly fragrant basil that grows to 24” tall. Bright green leaves contrast large, wine red flower clusters that fade to a dusty plum. Beneficial to pollinators, aroma deters common garden pests. 🌱💚

The plant with the blue-green leaves is, surprisingly to me, actually a fern! Coffee fern/coffee cliffbrake. It grows on rocky outcrops in the chaparral and other drier environments, and its leaves change from green to purplish red as they age (fern in the background is a polypody of some sort) 🌿

Crocus speciosus
Aka: Bieberstein's crocus
Native to northern and central Turkey, the Caucasus, northern Iran, Crimea and Bulgaria
A bulbous perennial that flowers without leaves in autumn, leaves are produced after flowering in the spring
Multiplies quickly

I planted this Fuschia in the ground last summer because it got aphids and I just wanted it to live or die. It inexplicably overwintered (zone 6), regrew, and bloomed beautifully. Now that I know it can, I'm going to mulch it and cover it with snow and see if we can make it through another winter! 🌱

Did you know we breed our own “black” sunflowers? Our Dark Chocolate sunflowers grow to 6ft with multiple branching heads, purple stems, and are uniformly darker than most chocolate varieties on the market.🌱💚🌻

A lovely old French iris from 1927, 'Floridor'. It has a wonderful, heavy grape fragrance, but its best feature is the unique shade of blue, often described as slate blue in the old catalogs. It really stands out in the garden and is such a good grower and bloomer.

Born in the tropical forests of Sri Lanka True (Ceylon) Cinnamon is well suited to grow in our zone 9b. This tree can grow up to 50 ft, or be pruned to live well in a container. It will not tolerate temperatures below 40 degrees, be sure to protect it from rare frosts. 🌱

Image via Tanria Elementals

Scrambled eggs! Scrambled eggs! 🍳 This cute native wildflower is often called “scrambled eggs” or “yellow Corydalis.” Across their native range you can find them blooming in and around rocky areas, creek beds, and hillsides from late winter through early fall as temperatures and conditions allow! 🌿

It’s remarkably easy to grow the most expensive spice on Earth in your home garden. 
Saffron grows from bulbs / corms. They bloom in the Fall and produce 3 threads that you want to carefully hand harvest on flowering day. Dry in parchment paper and place in an airtight jar. 🌱

Today's Gardening Advice: Siberian Iris Paprikash is one of the stranger Siberian Iris I have. The flower color is decidedly different. Goes well with other colors in the garden. Like other Iris relatives it prefers full sun and tolerates dry when established. Not picky about soil. 🌱

Once the primary 🌱 agricultural focus of the region, #Meghri #Armenia has has shifted away from Soviet-enforced pomegranate orchards to the more profitable (and perishable) persimmon.

Deprioritized, pomegranates still get packed - but no new orchards get planted. Legacy trees straggle along.

One of our patches of Blue-eyed grass is blooming nicely even though it has been cool and mostly cloudy lately. It looks like grass (except for the blooms) but is in the Iris family. 
2nd photo is a bloom closeup. 🌿 #NativePlants Southern California

Callicarpa bodinieri var. giraldii 'Profusion', Verbenaceae. The species is from E to Western China. This is in a North Yorkshire garden. Even on a dull day in low light, the fruits are startling - almost unreal

Start with a small act of anti capitalism.

Next time you buy a tomato, save the seeds. The slimy stuff on the seed is a sprout inhibiter. Put the seeds onto a tissue or paper kitchen towel. Save it. Dry it. Stash it somewhere dark. 

Next spring, soak in water and place in dirt and grow tomatoes

Two years ago when we moved here, this hydrangea was one single branch with a couple leaves. I thought it would not make it. The following summer, it had a tiny bloom. This year it has exploded in size and blossomed beautifully. We will have to move it this fall as it is crowding the gate 🌱

Last year’s dwarf irises—among my very favourite plants—were a treasure. This year we learn whether they’ll perennialize in the spots I picked for them, but as insurance, we planted another fifty. There can never be too many! They take up no space; their post-bloom foliage is narrow and subtle 🌱

Nasturtiums are a must for any garden. Beautiful, unusual, and fully edible. The bees also go nuts for them.

Not sold? They also love poor soil and will help fix it, great companion plants to deter pests, and perfect cut flowers.

🌺 nasturtiums 🌸 flowers 🧑🏼‍🌾 gardening 🌱 plants 🥗 harvest 💦 irrigation

This 2nd year hydrangea has more than doubled in size. The flowers are all fairly low on the bush (which is where the sun hits), and there's another foot of greenery at the top, out of the picture frame, which is its way of telling me that it wants to be transplanted forward this fall. 🌱

Two of my favorite plants for low-medium (sprawling) border color for the garden, the Erigeron karvinskianus – ‘Santa Barbara Daisy’ (1’-2’) white & pink flowers. Cuphea hybrid – ‘Starfire Cuphea’ is slightly taller (2’-3’) pinkish-red, glossy foliage & attracts hummingbirds. 🌱 #FlowerReport

Sunflowers may be the symbol of Ukraine, but they're native to North America, where they were first domesticated in the Late Woodland period.

Seeds found in Mexico are all pre-contact, suggesting Spanish priests suppressed them to block their importance in Sun-worship cults.

One thing I'm especially proud of with my garden is that I grow it almost entirely with reclaimed water. 💦

I live in CA where water conservation is a big deal, and we save every bit of water we can.

I have ~20 plants, and I've only watered with the hose twice this year.

The case for planting Allium: they are stunning and hardy. They are onion-family and a natural pest deterrent. They naturalize and multiply! They bring lovely height to a garden. Bees and butterflies adore them! 🌱
A suberized exodermis is required for tomato drought tolerance. Exciting, impactful work from the Brady lab and I’m delighted to have contributed to it! doi.org/10.1038/s414...

Putting creeping thyme (& other hardy groundcovers) on our little back patch of dirt, instead of grass, was great. 🌱

One-or-more of these are always blooming, most are hardy AF, bees buzz happily around and it needs maybe 1/10 the water 💦 or fertilizer. By mid-next-summer they’ll cover the gaps.

🌱Might as well sow all these basil seeds from the free packet I thought - there’s only 10. 
( ⏩they all germinate).

Been amazed at how prolific these modest size pepper plants have been this year. I pick all the fruit and a week or so later they’re back. I dont feed them or anything just water regularly.

Today's Gardening Advice: Certain wildflowers deserve to stay right in the woods.  Various orchids and trillium are difficult to establish in the average garden.  Orchids like the common lady slipper have symbiotic relationships with fungus.  Gardeners are too often tempted to try them and fail. 🌱

Considered by some to be the most important plant for pollination in North America, the Goldenrod (Solidago spp) is a gorgeous keystone species. Providing food for hundreds of insects and animals, this fall blooming flower can grow prolifically, even becoming weedy in some areas. 🌱

I've picked, roasted, and frozen the last of the cherry tomatoes. They got blight, thanks to the terrible weather, but they did OK. These tomatoes are ripening now in the small greenhouse. They're from seeds saved last year from shop-bought tomatoes and have done very well. 🌱
#Gardening #Tomatoes

Did you know saffron crocuses - which grow in your ordinary twenty-first-century garden - are basically clones of plants that grew in Minoan Crete, hundreds of years before Homer? Blows my mind. vertigrow.substack.com/p/saffron-cr...
Every year I plant a few less tomatoes (last year I had 700 cherry tomato plants, this year it's 650), every year my yield goes up faster than I planned and I end up with lots of surplus.

Corn plant propagation going well now that I've added an aquarium pump to the vases. Propagation station looking healthy. The schefflera, on the other hand, is getting cramped. Should have pruned it months ago. 🌱

DRAMA! I just went out to talk to my veggie garden. The cucumbers were STRANGLING the tomato plants!! The cucumbers have taken over my garden and wrapped their tendrils around  the neighbouring plants. NO SIRS. You back off.

I re-staked the tomato plants and told them everything was going to be ok.

First wildflower of…winter??? Texas baby-blue-eyes are native annuals distributed from Texas, Oklahoma, Louisiana, to Alabama. They generally have an early bloom season, starting from around mid/late February where winter temps are moderate, through the end of spring, and dying back by summer. 🌿

Ignore the fact my hand is all sweaty, I was finishing up a run lol…

Happy to report that it’s Texas persimmon (Diospyros texana) season! Persimmon fruits ripen around early August and are a fav among wildlife. TX persimmons have a small range from central and west TX through northeastern Mexico. 🧪

Sowed 4 different peppers 🌶️ and aubergine ‘Violetta di Firenze’, they are on a heat mat set to 22 degrees C in a propagator with dome. (That’s why the picture is a bit blurry.)
It should take about five days for the first signs of germination 🌱

My 15-year-old jade plant recently went through a rough patch where she looked horrible— shriveled, drooping, discolored. Did you know that your plants’ soil can become hydrophobic? Well, a good repotting took care of that, and now my girl is back to her old self. Much to my relief.

The winter rains turned my yard of mulch into a meadow, and the first California native plants are starting to bloom! Closeups: wartleaf ceanothus, pitcher sage, and blueblossom. (If you're just tuning in, this was a grass lawn 3 years ago)

🌱 Perennial hollyhocks (Alcalthaea suffrutescens "Parkallee & "Parkfrieden") in the morning light of the autumn equinox. They bloom from July until October (sometimes longer) and are very popular with wild bees.

A little Sunday trivia for ya:

I love Trillium 🤍 seeing them means spring is coming. It’s also Wisconsin’s State Flower. Don’t pick them because doing so can disrupt the plant’s ability to sustain itself and it can take years to bloom again- once established they can live 100 years!

Today's Gardening Advice: Hemerocallis "Katahdin" is one of my favorite daylilies. Tall scapes, prolific flowers, and grows well. A Ron Valente introduction, it is cold hardy and named for a mountain in Maine. Not the most fancy flower, but a robust, dependable performer. 🌱

My single cucumber plant may take over the world. It’s now over 6’ tall and 4 feet wide after training it up the lattice. The huge bushy tomato plants pale beside it. You can barely see the corn or peas at all. This is shaping up to be a banner garden year. There must be over 100 cucumber flowers! 🌱

My mother’s rose - Munstead Wood. Mum grew this in a pot by her back door. Now it is beside my back door, repotted and thriving.

It smells as glorious as it looks.

Some lilacs are tolerant of heat and can flourish (zone-pusher) in the hot inland valleys of Southern California, with just that right location. “Now that the lilacs are in bloom, she has a bowl of lilacs in her room.” - T.S. Eliot 🌱#GardeningFeed

Spent a wet Sunday planting 250 flaxes into the second half of our wetland project. Most still need their PJs on :-) Still a few to go, but we should get close to 1000 trees when it’s all done.

Torrential rain here today so I spent it in the greenhouse. Sowed thyme, basil, brussels sprouts, peas, tomatoes, aubergines, broccoli and agastache. I also pricked out lettuce, sunflowers and heleniums. Busy busy! 🌱😊

Recently, I have been looking into the Late Pleistocene climate and vegetation of Argentina for a work project, and was delighted to find out that pink peppercorn was - and still is - part of the native flora in the area. Maybe Macrauchenia developed a taste for the spicy tree.

🌱 The wild columbine is blooming all over the garden. A native which is not planted so much as exuberantly spreads itself by seeds. Thyme is exceptionally easy to grow. It needs well draining soil and full sun, and loves to be trimmed. Waiting until just before flowering ensures the leaves have the most oil available. Our rainy season can damage the plant, but moving it indoors or away from the elements can help. 🌱

🌱 The perennial sunflowers are starting to bloom. It's nice to see them come back for the 2nd yr. Had to deadhead aggressively last year as I didn't want it taking over the yard.I'm still hoping to find some Mexican sunflowers. Not as tall, comes back every yearly & can be planted as a nice hedge.

Aubergines in the greenhouse are finally getting going which is much appreciated but somewhat pointless given it's mid-October.

Today's Gardening Advice: Check seed packet date ranges prior to starting seedlings.  Some vegetable seeds germinate quickly and grow just as fast.  Too often I hear from people, "Oh, I started my tomatoes in January and now they're all leggy." Yes, wait, read directions, don't start too soon. 🌱

Fleabane daisies are really quite weedy plants, but I am continuing to like them very much. Also enjoying the aromatic asters, now that they've made it thru an entire summer of me wondering repeatedly if they were something I planted on purpose.

🌱Due to less rain and higher temperatures, I mow the grass only twice a year (also good for insects and #birds) and I planted mostly mediterranean plants and herbs (olive, thyme, sage, lavender...). They need less water and are beautiful.🍃
Btw: Sage and thyme reproduce by themselves through seeds.

Higher ed is the most poorly reported on sector of society. Journalists think that because they went to college, they understand how universities function. Hence, & in contrast to how other industries are covered, journalists don’t defer to those who work in universities to shape reporting.

Here’s my non-helpful take: when socioeconomic inequality is high, and higher education stratification is steep (specially when institutional position is determined partly by undergraduate admissions), there is no way to technique your way out of privileged students’ domination of privileged places.

How bad is the lack of socio-economic diversity in economics? Read our Q&A with Stone Center Affiliated Scholar Anna Stansbury of MIT Sloan about how economics compares with 14 major disciplines on 1st-generation college grads and how this intersects with race.
bit.ly/48yZPTt

My research team's newest article is out in Educational Researcher. We examined the extent to which different state funding models are associated with college access and success. Short answer: not much, which aligns with research on performance funding.

Success as an academic selects for both obedience and individualism, this essay suggests, a deadly combination for solidarity across ranks of the academic labor force. Secure academics, on this view, are as much to blame as admin/management for the deterioration of labor conditions in higher ed.

Many people are talking about the NYT's obsession with the Ivy League, which reminds me of the research paper showing 1 in 5 employees in 2016 were Ivy League grads and the AAJA Voices investigation showing how this elitism affects its internships: theintercept.com/2018/05/06/n...

I don't know how you can be a PhD program when you offer your PhD students no teaching opportunities (i.e., a chance to learn part of the job) and no funding support for conference travel (i.e., closing off another part of the job). This has been a subtweet.

One underrated aspect of the WVU debacle is that tenure and shared governance have been de facto abolished there. Faculty can be disciplined for "speaking out against the greater good." The university claims the right to fire tenured faculty basically at will.

i don’t think it’s a huge exaggeration to say that the ‘shortage’ of funding for higher education (like other funding shortages) is a political not an economic reality, the result of a wildly successful propaganda campaign by the anti-keynesianism of a bunch of think tanks funded by billionaires

These stories never tell you what is actually happening, which is emphatically NOT that humanities are disappearing from higher ed, but rather that public ed is being converted into vocational training for a working class while elite private universities for the rich will remain universities.

I have often wondered why bullying is so common in academia. Is it because it is so dependent on personal relationships,  patronage and reputation. Or because it is normalized in its everyday functioning? Or because it is deeply hierarchical under its liberal facade? I think all of it is true.

Headline: Degree in Magic to be offered at prominent UK university 

Half the internet, including a headteacher famous for her supposed emphasis on academic rigour: the world has lost its mind!!!

The other half, checking the facts: it’s… an MA in history and sociology…

The fact that the apparently only viable way to get a salary increase in academia is to get an outside offer is such a problem especially when considering how time intensive academic searches are for all involved.

one underrated factor here is that the Ivy League makes its students feel very Important and Meaningful (in mostly good ways! it's harder and better teaching) than Oxbridge does, which sustains the belief in the best and the brightest, etc.

Imagine being one of the richest universities in the world and advertising PhD-requiring fellowships for <£28k. And then saying you care about equality & diversity amongst academics. I hesitate to even post these on the jobs wiki, it’s so embarrassing (and the jobs are obscure and badly explained)
Two things can be true at once: 1) DEI is largely a corporate box checking exercise that has little to do with actually addressing structural racism and 2) it drives actual racists insane with anger.

The University of Florida cut all of its staff positions relating to diversity, equity and inclusion and shuttered the Office of the Chief Diversity Officer to comply with a state mandate, the school announced in a memo on Friday.

Here are two things I want people with permanent jobs to know about being a precarious academic:
1. I have never had pay rise.

Dir. David Lynch
United States
Avant-Garde, Horror, Cult
